title: Sen. Harckham, Asm. Woerner, Advocates Urge Support for Clean Fuel Standard Bill
content: New York State Senator Pete Harckham and Assemblymember Carrie Woerner, along with leaders from environmental and agricultural groups, labor and the Clean Fuels coalition, urged support for the Clean Fuel Standard Act of 2025 (CFS) at a press conference today held at the State Capitol. The Clean Fuel Standard Act of 2025 (S.1343A / A.472), which was introduced by State Sen. Kevin S. Parker and Asm. Woerner, will reduce on-road transportation sector greenhouse gas emissions, accelerate New Yorkâ€™s shift to clean energy, and help build a more affordable and equitable future for all New Yorkers. The legislation will not only reduce emissions and support working families but will also serve as a key driver of New Yorkâ€™s electric vehicle (EV) transition by incentivizing investments in EV charging infrastructure.
author: Analysts Account
source: New York State Senator Pete Harckham
